title: 英國當局褫奪曾加入「伊斯蘭國」女子公民身份

replies.msg:
英國當局褫奪曾加入「伊斯蘭國」女子公民身份

https://news.rthk.hk/rthk/ch/component/k2/1443737-20190220.htm

英國女子貝居姆4年前到敘利亞加入極端組織「伊斯蘭國」，她目前在敘利亞的難民營，早前剛誕下嬰兒，並要求回國，據報英國當局已經褫奪貝居姆的公民身份。

英國傳媒報道，貝居姆的家人收到內政部信件，指已經發出褫奪貝居姆公民身份的命令，又指貝居姆有權作出上訴。

貝居姆的代表律師透露，貝居姆的家人對此感到非常失望，正研究透過所有法律途徑挑戰當局的決定。

I think the recurring theme among these comments of "peak Guardian" hits the nail on the head. While I consider myself to be centrist/centre-left and have no love whatsoever for far-right Islamophobes, and while I hold the Guardian to generally be a respectable news outlet, its op-eds tend to stretch the definition of "bleeding-heart liberalism" to ridiculous limits.

For what it's worth, I agree that Ms Begum should be given a chance to redeem herself. But the keyword here is "redeem", not "rehabilitate". She was 15 when she was supposedly "groomed". That's old enough for most teenagers to know what they're letting themselves in for. If there was a 15-year-old who was picked up by a far-right group and trained to prepare for bombings on mosques, synagogues and journalists, the Guardian would be encouraging a very different narrative. At 15, I knew the difference between right and wrong. If I was egged on by neo-nazis at 15 to beat up anyone who looked Middle-Eastern, I'm pretty sure I'd known to have told them where to stick it.

Everyone has a right to earn forgiveness. But the greater the sin, the greater the effort involving in earning it. She hasn't done herself any favours by telling the world that she has "no regrets" and that seeing severed heads in bins didn't faze her.

If her interest in going back to the UK is genuinely for the welfare of her child, and she's willing to give that child up for adoption or care (possibly by her own family), then I'm sure a solution can be found. After all, that child has some claim to UK/Dutch nationality and has a right to protection and to a safe life. As long as it's not exposed to the same extremist ideologies.

But if she's envisaging coming back to a life where everyone pretends that nothing happened just out of sympathy for a supposedly poor, defenceless mother and her baby, well, I think the proper expression here is "get fucked". Anyone who gives comfort to the enemy, especially one as depraved, inhuman and vile as Islamic State, must face consequences, especially if they're unrepentant.
